reasoning: Virgin Galactic possessed roughly $1.1 billion in cash and marketable securities as of Q3 2023, while burning slightly over $100 million per quarter. The company announced strategic cost-cutting measures, including layoffs and a pause in Unity commercial flights, specifically to ensure their cash runway extends into 2026 when their Delta-class ships are expected to generate revenue. While aerospace development is notoriously prone to delays and Virgin Galactic's sister company Virgin Orbit already went bankrupt, Virgin Galactic's substantial initial cash cushion and lack of imminent debt maturities (convertible notes mature in 2027) make an actual bankruptcy filing before January 1, 2026 relatively unlikely. Management will likely stretch their runway as far as possible, potentially via further cuts or additional equity offerings, limiting the probability of a Chapter 11 filing before the end of 2025.
